card.intuition: Using visualization tools (like Webpack Bundle Analyzer) to see exactly which libraries and files are taking up the most space in your final JavaScript bundle.
card.analogy: Checking your credit card statement: you might be surprised to see that 'Small Subscription' (that tiny library you imported) is actually costing you $50/month (adding 500KB to your bundle).
when_to_use: Finding 'Heavy' libraries that can be replaced with lighter alternatives
gotchas: Make sure you are analyzing the PRODUCTION bundle, not the development one. Dev bundles are much larger and not minified
Look for 'Parsed' size vs 'Gzipped' size. Gzipped size is what the user actually downloads, but Parsed size is what the browser's CPU has to process

exercise.prompt: Identify the name of the popular optimization tool used to visualize JS bundles as a 'Treemap'.
